name: Wonders of the World
description: Famous landmarks, natural wonders, and iconic sites

questions.q: The Great Wall is located in which country?

source.notes: Stretches more than 13,000 miles across northern China.

questions.q: The Pyramids of Giza are located in which country?

source.notes: On the outskirts of modern-day Cairo.

questions.q: In which city would you visit the Eiffel Tower?

source.notes: Built for the 1889 Exposition Universelle; designed by Gustave Eiffel's firm.

questions.q: In which U.S. city is the Statue of Liberty located?

source.notes: Stands on Liberty Island in New York Harbor; a gift from France dedicated in 1886.

questions.q: The Taj Mahal, a white-marble mausoleum, is in which country?

source.notes: Located in Agra; commissioned by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in 1632.

questions.q: The Colosseum, a massive 1st-century amphitheatre, is in which European city?

source.notes: Completed in AD 80 under Emperor Titus.

questions.q: The Christ the Redeemer statue overlooks which South American city?

source.notes: Atop Mount Corcovado; completed in 1931.

questions.q: What is the longest river in the world?

source.notes: About 6,650 km long; runs through 11 African countries.

questions.q: Angel Falls, the world's tallest uninterrupted waterfall, is located in which country?

source.notes: Drops 979 metres in Canaima National Park, Venezuela.

questions.q: In which year was the Eiffel Tower officially completed?

source.notes: Built as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair.

questions.q: The Kremlin is the fortified historic complex at the heart of which city?

source.notes: Overlooks Red Square; the seat of the Russian government.

questions.q: Michelangelo's Sistine Chapel ceiling is located in which sovereign state?

source.notes: Ceiling painted by Michelangelo between 1508 and 1512.

questions.q: The Forbidden City, a vast imperial palace complex, is located in which city?

source.notes: Home to 24 Ming and Qing emperors between 1420 and 1912.

questions.q: The Acropolis, featuring the Parthenon temple, overlooks which capital city?

source.notes: Built in the 5th century BC under Pericles.

questions.q: What is the largest desert on Earth, when measured by land area?

source.notes: Antarctica is a polar desert — the largest desert by area at ~14 million km².

questions.q: The Great Pyramid of Giza is primarily built from which stone?

source.notes: Cased with Tura limestone; core blocks are local limestone.

questions.q: The Brandenburg Gate is located in which capital?

source.notes: Completed 1791; the only remaining gate of Berlin's old city wall.

questions.q: The Alhambra, a spectacular Moorish palace and fortress, is located in which country?

source.notes: Located in Granada, Spain; completed in 1358.

questions.q: The ancient Lighthouse of Alexandria, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, was located in modern-day which country?

source.notes: Built on Pharos Island around 280 BC; destroyed by earthquakes between 956 and 1323 AD.

questions.q: The Statue of Zeus, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, was located at which Greek site?

source.notes: Sculpted by Phidias c. 435 BC; destroyed in late antiquity.

questions.q: What destroyed the Colossus of Rhodes, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World?

source.notes: Destroyed by an earthquake in 226 BC after standing for 54 years.

questions.q: The Uffizi Gallery, one of Europe's most important art museums, is located in which city?

source.notes: Housed in a 16th-century palazzo commissioned by Cosimo I de' Medici.
